analysis.consistency: The feature name 'Dueling' aligns well with the description of gaining a bonus when wielding a single melee weapon (a common theme for duelist archetypes). The class association ('ranger') is noted, although Dueling is traditionally a fighting style often associated with martial classes.
analysis.quality: The description is clear, concise, and mechanically specific. It clearly states the condition (one-handed melee weapon, no other weapons) and the benefit (+2 damage rolls).
analysis.mechanics: This is a direct application of the 'Dueling' fighting style, granting a flat bonus to damage rolls. This is a strong, straightforward mechanical benefit that scales well with character progression, making it a desirable choice for melee-focused rangers.
analysis.notes: The feature is marked as `is_subclass_feature: true`, suggesting it might be a subclass feature of a specific Ranger archetype, rather than a core class feature/fighting style available to all Rangers at level 1. Mechanically, it mimics the standard 'Dueling' fighting style found in many tabletop role-playing systems.

data.name: Dueling
data.description: When you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.
